title: Sour Silky Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, tangy lamb tagine with a silky broth, perfect for a solo keto dinner.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the lamb cubes and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add the remaining olive oil, diced onion, and minced garlic; sauté until the onion is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste, lemon juice, white wine vinegar, and red wine; bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits.
Return the lamb to the pot, add the caraway seeds, cumin, coriander, and turmeric; stir to combine.
Add enough water to cover the lamb by roughly 1 cm (about 250 ml). Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and let the tagine simmer for 60–80 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the broth has thickened.
Fold in the sliced olives and chopped parsley; season with salt and pepper to taste.
Taste the sauce and adjust acidity with a splash more lemon juice or vinegar if needed.
Serve hot, spooning the silky, sour broth over the tender lamb.
Optionally garnish with extra parsley and a drizzle of olive oil before serving.
